steps.title: Identify the vertex from vertex form
steps.content: The function $f(x) = \frac{1}{9}(x-6)^2 + 3$ is in vertex form $f(x) = a(x-h)^2 + k$ with $h=6$ and $k=3$, so the vertex is $(6, 3)$. The $x$-coordinate $6$ represents seconds after the car started moving and the $y$-coordinate $3$ represents the height in inches.

steps.title: Determine whether the vertex is a minimum or maximum
steps.content: Since $a = \frac{1}{9} > 0$, the parabola opens upward, so the vertex is the lowest point on the graph. This means the car's minimum height is $3$ inches, and it occurs at $x = 6$ seconds (not at the start).

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A: "Minimum height was 3 inches." Correct — the vertex $y$-value $3$ is the minimum since the parabola opens up.
  • B: "Minimum height was 6 inches." Incorrect — $6$ is the $x$-coordinate (time in seconds), not the height.
  • C: "Height was 3 inches when it started moving." Incorrect — at $x=0$, $f(0) = \frac{1}{9}(36)+3 = 7$, not $3$.
  • D: "Height was 6 inches when it started moving." Incorrect — $f(0) = 7$ inches, not $6$, and $6$ refers to time at the vertex.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
